title: Events, Observability, Capacity, Inventory, and Cost Control · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers events, observability, capacity, inventory, and cost control into a new storage telemetry, inventory, capacity, and cost model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of storage telemetry, inventory, capacity, and cost model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.
Correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.
Measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Delivery Semantics
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 2742 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to handle duplicate unordered events with bounded telemetry cardinality and cost; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Delivery Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for consume s3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Delivery Semantics, keep Event Delivery Semantics connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Capacity Cost Alerts
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-capacity-cost-alerts-1d93v4" that uses case 3322 to handle duplicate unordered events with bounded telemetry cardinality and cost. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
